hypothesis.thesis_statement: Paper 'Sentiment Feedback in Equity Markets: Asymmetries, Retail Heterogeneity, and Structural Calibration' (arXiv:2509.11970) proposes a signal in the 'behavioral_chips' dimension. Hypothesis: an A-share adaptation predicts the cross-section of forward returns. Test whether the published effect ports to A-shares.
hypothesis.mechanism: Taxonomy dimension: behavioral_chips (FRONTIER_OPEN). Buildable on: chip_distribution(cyq_perf); price_ohlcv(market_daily). Abstract: We study how sentiment shocks propagate through equity returns and investor clientele using four independent proxies with sign-aligned kappa-rho parameters. A structural calibration links a one standard deviation innovation in sentiment to a pricing impact of 1.06 basis points with persistence parameter rho = 0.940, yielding a half-life of 11.2 months. source.title: Sentiment Feedback in Equity Markets: Asymmetries, Retail Heterogeneity, and Structural Calibration

pre_registered_concerns.most_likely_failure_mode: [DRAFT] The paper's effect may not survive A-share microstructure (T+1, limit boards, retail flow) or may be subsumed by existing factors after neutralization.
pre_registered_concerns.weakest_assumption: [DRAFT] That the paper's construction maps faithfully to Tushare A-share fields without definitional drift.
pre_registered_concerns.what_would_falsify_this: [DRAFT] Sealed-OOS rank-ICIR below the committed band, or hard rules failing after realistic costs.
pre_registered_concerns.priors_on_cost_sensitivity: [DRAFT] Review turnover at the chosen rebalance.

arxiv_evidence_context_only.abstract: We study how sentiment shocks propagate through equity returns and investor clientele using four independent proxies with sign-aligned kappa-rho parameters. A structural calibration links a one standard deviation innovation in sentiment to a pricing impact of 1.06 basis points with persistence parameter rho = 0.940, yielding a half-life of 11.2 months. The impulse response peaks around the 12-month horizon, indicating slow-moving amplification. Cross-sectionally, a simple D10-D1 portfolio earns 4.0 basis points per month with Sharpe ratios of 0.18-0.85, consistent with tradable exposure to the sentiment factor. Three regularities emerge: (i) positive sentiment innovations transmit more stron…

todo_before_registering: Read the full paper; extract the EXACT signal construction.
Implement 'arxiv_behavioral_chips_2509_11970' in factor_library mapping it to A-share Tushare/Qlib fields (PIT-safe Ref(...,1)); confirm fields are registered/approved.
Run the SANDBOX screen + size/industry-neutralized MARGINAL-contribution test vs the catalog (marginal IC × low correlation — the house rule), NOT standalone ICIR.
SET expected_effect = YOUR A-share prediction (point + CI + horizon).
CONFIRM the OOS window is unburned, then factor_lifecycle (draft→candidate) → sealed-OOS.
